patch.value: GH-490: unknown-command suggester ranks substring hits over synonyms/edit distance — pm log suggests 'extension catalog'

## Source
GitHub issue #490 (version 2026.7.9).

## Reproduction
```bash
pm log some-id "a note"
# Error: Unknown command log
# Next steps: Did you mean: extension catalog, package catalog?
```

## Problem
The suggester ranks by substring containment ("log" ⊂ "cataLOG"), so both catalog subcommands outrank the commands an agent actually means — `history`, `comments`, `notes`. An agent following the hint is misdirected to the extension catalog instead of the item-history surface.

## Proposed ranking
1. Alias/synonym table for the top verbs agents guess (log→history/comments/notes, ls→list, rm→delete, …)
2. Edit distance on the command token
3. Substring hits last

Even a small hardcoded synonym table removes most misdirection. The rest of the unknown-command error contract (structured JSON, recovery bundle, exit 2) is good — only the ranking misleads.

## Why it matters for agents
Stateless agents guess verbs constantly; suggestion quality determines whether recovery costs one retry or a full `pm --help` roundtrip (96 lines) plus re-orientation. Related: pm-gdi7 covers the unknown-OPTION flag-suggestion gap; this covers unknown-COMMAND ranking.

pm log suggests history/comments/notes ahead of catalog commands; suggester ranks synonym table then edit distance then substring; unit tests cover top-10 verb synonyms; unknown-command JSON contract shape unchanged
